items.summary: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

items.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder in Canberra
items.summary: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

items.title: 'Second wave' of algal bloom forecast for SA's Spencer Gulf
items.summary: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

items.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
items.summary: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

items.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
items.summary: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
items.summary: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

items.title: Regional employers, academics say one-size-fits-all migration policy will decimate critical workforces
items.summary: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

items.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying
items.summary: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
items.summary: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

items.title: One in 10 Australian teens relying on withdrawal method and half not using condoms as STIs rise
items.summary: A long-running survey of Australian teens has identified falling condom use and unwanted sex as major concerns for young people starting relationships.

items.title: Upper house member Ruth Forrest calls for apology after personal health request leaked to media
items.summary: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.

items.title: Labor's capital gains tax challenge deepens as biotech sector raises alarm
items.summary: Another sector has called for a capital gains tax change carve-out, warning the government that it will face a talent drain without it.

items.title: Torquay artist's willie wagtail paintings taking flight around the Surf Coast and beyond
items.summary: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

items.title: State of Origin: Ethan Strange and James Tedesco star in Blues' 22-20 win over Maroons
items.summary: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

items.title: Australia ranks third in the world for battery capacity and WA is leading the charge
items.summary: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

items.title: Labor is quietly confident the battery boom will help prevent power price shocks
items.summary: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

items.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave
items.summary: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

items.title: Put your Australian TV show knowledge to the test with this picture quiz
items.summary: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

items.title: Explain the Internet: The Backrooms captured cyberspace and now it's infecting cinemas
items.summary: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

items.title: New York and New Jersey investigate 2026 FIFA World Cup ticket prices
items.summary: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

items.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals
items.summary: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

items.title: State of Origin: Kalyn Ponga shattered by send-off in Game I as Ashley Klein overrules bunker
items.summary: Kalyn Ponga is left feeling responsible for Queensland's Origin I collapse as the decision to send him off leaves several greats shocked.

items.title: State of Origin quick hits: James Tedesco scores Blues' match-winner, Maroons star Kalyn Ponga sent off
items.summary: The Blues leave it late, the Maroons finish with 12 players, while the bunker draws the ire of players, commentators and spectators alike. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin I.

items.title: NASA reveals plans for living on the moon

items.title: NT police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody in Alice Springs still unknown
items.summary: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Death in custody of Patience Kuzmins leaves family heartbroken, inquest hears
items.summary: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

items.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
items.summary: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

items.title: Whyalla locals call for more certainty around steelworks' future amid news of final bidders
items.summary: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

items.title: Maroons let 20-0 lead slip in Origin I after Kalyn Ponga sent off late
items.summary: James Tedesco scores a spectacular try with 90 seconds left in State of Origin I, capping off a 20-point comeback after Queensland fullback Kalyn Ponga is sent off for a shoulder charge.

items.title: Labor MP who unseated Peter Dutton defends electoral commission referral over empty block
items.summary: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

items.title: Albany bakery cops fines over $60,000 after inspectors find rodent faeces and animal blood on bread
items.summary: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread, rodent faeces on food and spreading mould at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

items.title: Maugean skates released back into Macquarie Harbour after two years in captivity
items.summary: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight
items.summary: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

items.title: Antisemitism victims abused online after giving evidence at royal commission into Bondi terror attack
items.summary: Victims of antisemitism have been targeted with death threats and depicted as animals in AI-generated images after giving evidence to the royal commission examining the Bondi terror attack.

items.title: Drought-affected farmers welcome significant rainfall but some miss out

items.title: Billions are traded each week on Kalshi and Polymarket. There is a push to bring prediction markets to Australia
items.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

items.title: Jim Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business capital gains tax debate
items.summary: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.

items.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike during Iran war
items.summary: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

items.summary: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

items.title: Councils responsible for more than 'rates, roads and rubbish' as Geraldton LGA buys land from state
items.summary: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

items.title: Measles cases rise in Australia as authorities monitor deadly outbreaks in South and South-East Asia
items.summary: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

items.title: The government's proposed changes to the unemployment system, explained
items.summary: The federal government has proposed changes to Australia's $2 billion employment services system to make them “more responsive, effective and dignified”.

items.title: Adelaide track designer estimates MotoGP remodel could cost more than $100m
items.summary: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

items.title: Cuts of meat that can save you money and how to cook with them
items.summary: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

items.title: Allegedly stolen cars cause peak hour traffic chaos in Cairns
items.summary: Police have arrested four teenage boys after three allegedly stolen cars caused traffic chaos across several Cairns suburbs today.

items.title: BOM plans to build new radar at Tennant Creek airport, 11 years after closure
items.summary: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

items.title: Since placing his first bet at age 15, Dylan says he's lost at least $900k
items.summary: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teens gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

items.title: Deputy police chief held 'reservations' over expanding powers of Jewish security groups
items.summary: NSW Police Deputy Commissioner David Hudson has given evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

items.title: Concussion saw 15-year-old Macy Watson lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover
items.summary: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.

items.title: Lord Howe Island invertebrates boom after rodent eradication
items.summary: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.

items.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work
items.summary: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

items.title: Headline inflation eases to 4.2 per cent in April as fuel prices fall
items.summary: Headline inflation decelerated in April, with consumer prices increasing at an annual pace of 4.2 per cent, down from 4.6 per cent in March.

items.title: Scholarship to be created in memory of farmer Callum Smail after death at 26
items.summary: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

items.title: Alexia Putellas ends 14-season Barcelona career after fourth Champions League title
items.summary: Alexia Putellas ends her stint at Barcelona after winning 10 league titles and four Champions Leagues with the Spanish giants.

items.title: Australia's Kimberly Birrell stuns Jessica Pegula as Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka dazzle at French Open
items.summary: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

items.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest
items.summary: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

items.title: Trailblazing female driving instructor's extraordinary legacy in South Australia's Riverland
items.summary: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.

items.title: Government pitches largest overhaul to unemployment system in decades
items.summary: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

items.title: St John Ambulance NT considering body-worn cameras amid ongoing assaults on paramedics
items.summary: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

items.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley says Nathan Cleary unfairly judged over losing State of Origin record
items.summary: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

items.summary: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

items.title: Australian Adam Walton stuns former world number one Daniil Medvedev at French Open
items.summary: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

items.title: Peer support worker Brandie McGrady is a Dubbo Drug Court success story
